text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Egg consumption affects blood pressure","claimType":"empirical","quote":"\"Effect of Egg Consumption on Blood Pressure: a Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Clinical Trials\"","entailment":"The title explicitly states that the review examined the effect of egg consumption on blood pressure, indicating a causal relationship investigated in the study.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"blood pressure","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Egg consumption is associated title indicates that the meta‑analysis evaluated the association between egg consumption and hypertension risk, implying an observational relationship.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"hypertension risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption is associated with risk of coronary heart disease and stroke","claimType":"empirical","quote":"\"Meta-analysis of Egg Consumption and Risk of Coronary Heart Disease and Stroke\"","entailment":"The title reports that a meta‑analysis examined the relationship between egg consumption and the risk of coronary heart disease and stroke, suggesting an associative link.","population":"","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"coronary heart disease and stroke risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption is related to risk of coronary heart disease, stroke, and heart failure","claimType":"empirical","quote":"\"Food groups and risk of coronary heart disease, stroke and heart failure: A systematic review and dose‑response meta‑analysis of prospective studies\"","entailment":"The title conveys that the systematic review and dose‑response meta‑analysis investigated how food groups, including eggs, relate to the risk of
